name: Shrimp Tempura
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix cake flour with eggs and ice water until batter is slightly lumpy. Chill.
2. To make sauce, combine soy sauce, mirin, water, and MSG in a saucepan and bring to boil.
3. Place small amounts of sauce in tiny saucers with 1 teaspoon of grated radish on each. Set aside.
4. Shell and devein shrimp, leaving the tail intact. Flatten slightly with a sturdy whack of a cleaver or flat side of a heavy knife so they will not curl while cooking.
5. Arrange shrimp, mushrooms, eggplant, celery, carrots, sweet potatoes, and green beans attractively on a large tray or platter.
6. Heat oil in a deep kettle to 350°F (175°C).
7. Beat the batter.
8. Dip shrimp into all-purpose flour, then into chilled batter, shaking to remove excess batter. Slip into deep fat and fry until shrimp rise to surface.
9. While shrimp are bobbing on the surface of the oil, dollop a bit more batter on top of each shrimp and cook until batter is crisp and slightly golden.
10. Turn once and remove with a slotted spoon or fork and drain on wire rack. Keep hot.
11. Dip vegetables in flour and batter and cook in the same manner.
12. Continue to cook and drain shrimp and vegetables, a few at a time, adjusting heat during cooking to maintain 350°F (175°C) temperature.
13. Keep batter cool.
14. To serve, place an equal amount of various foods on each plate. Dip cooked vegetables and shrimp into sauce, scooping up a bit of radish.
